title_guess: Wales: Upper Cantref Farm, Brecon (May half-term)

pages.text:
We had supper at Jonathan's on the way - broccoli, cheddar, Stilton, Butterscotch icecream + vanilla sauce.
    Wordsworth was left £900 by a friend whom he nursed while he died of consumption. Wordsworth invested the money, to receive £80 p.a., of which £8 pa was paid in rent for Dove Cottage. The Grasmere vicar earned £40 pa at this time.

Saturday May 27th
    We left, the 7 of us at 8.20, at 9.0 we stopped for breakfast on Bewdley by-pass. It's advertised as a car park for the Worcs. Way. We drove on along roads, not motorways, through very heavy traffic + only arrived at __Upper Cantref Farm__ at 12.00. The boys played cricket. Peter + [?Thomas] rode in the afternoon. It started raining just as William + Simon finished putting up the tents. Simon + Eve + John walked down to [the village] [?Llangynach]. It is extraordinarily windy + [Simon]

kept sitting up in bed to check the tents. John slept in the cab + Charles slept on the top bunk for the first time. John + Charles are the same age that William + Peter were when first we started camping.
__Sunday 28th May__
	At 9.30 John + Charles rode around the ring on leading reins led by the rest of us. John started learning to post to the trot.
	At 10.30. Thomas, William + Peter set off on a day trip. It didn't stop raining. At 2.40. they came back without having stopped for a picnic lunch.
	Eve, John + Charles went off to Evensong + Thomas + William had a fight in the gangway + broke William's glasses.
	There was a sexton, his wife + the vicar + we sang hymns unaccompanied.
	After tea the sun came out, we tried to dry the clothes + played cricket.
	John + Charles had baths in the shower [illegible]. Peter + Eve showered.
__Monday__ Thomas + [William crossed out]Peter [?William written above] showered in the morning. It's still raining a lot. There are ewes lambing in this field. The farmer woman comes to visit them every few hours. Before lunch one

of the ewes had one lamb, + after lunch there were two.	John + Charles went on horseback in the ring [?with a leading rein].
	Thomas + Peter set off on a day ride with Jean, the head horse woman (really a local school teacher) William, John, Charles, [?Junior] + Eve set off to Brecon. We stopped at the White Swan __Llanfrynach__, to book a meal of 7 at 7.. At Brecon we telephoned Joan Clarke, cancelling the barbeque + inviting ourselves to tea tomorrow. We bought good-quality waterproofs for T, W, P. We bought charcoal, bread + milk. We should have brought one loaf per day, + we had forgotten the tinned milk. On the way back to Cantref we stopped in the road to look at the Blackham's house.
	Having started lunch outside, we moved the table into the tent as it started to shower. In the afternoon the boys played cricket with Simon + Charles + Eve collected sheep pooh.
	At 6.15 we set off to walk the 1½ miles to the restaurant. We got there early, + followed a sort of mill-race back through a park towards [to] a Roman Bathhouse.
	Supper was delicious - onion soup, Welsh lamb chops + caramel cream. We staggered home.

Tuesday 30th May
	No rain so far this morning. John wanders up to look at the horses. He loves the dogs & cats. There is a dog, corgi, called Holly. All 7 of us are setting out on a ride at 10.30.
	The ride was marvellous. There were 11 of us altogether. We went along trails & narrow stream beds & hillsides. Twice one of the guides went ahead with John, Charles & Simon & the rest of us held back for a canter. John has already learnt to rise to the trot & Charles bounces about like a relaxed pea. There were lovely views & it didn't rain.
	Unfortunately William fell off onto his nose on the last canter. (Superman fell off the day before & broke his neck).
	We had lunch, packed up & drove off to have tea with Joan Clarke. Then we went on for fish & chips at Malvern & dropped Thomas. We got home, showered the children & Eve was in the bath when John W & Hannah & 3 boys arrived!
	Inspite of the rain, it was a super half-term camping trip. (& 200 for camping & riding)
